title: Finite Backward Euler Method Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite backward Euler bad implicit-step QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational backward Euler replay for y' = -y with h = 1/2 computes first next_state = 2/3, while a malformed row claims next_state = 1/2, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional implicit time-stepping rows only when they add distinct nonlinear solve, fixed-point, Newton iteration, stiffness, floating-point, or convergence-theorem pressure; keep general implicit-method stability theory in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed ODE, step size, endpoint derivatives, implicit residuals, state updates, and monotone rational trace as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false first backward Euler step through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact implicit next state.
Expose the pack through finite dynamics/time-stepping, numerical-analysis, real-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general implicit-method convergence, stability, stiffness behavior, nonlinear solves, adaptive methods, and floating-point time-stepping correctness marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
